seoTitle: Queens Park vs Quelagetting — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Queens Park and Quelagetting. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Queens Park vs Quelagetting: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Queens Park and Quelagetting.
heading: Queens Park vs Quelagetting — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Queens Park, Western Australia and Quelagetting, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Queens Park has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Quelagetting (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Queens Park or Quelagetting safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Queens Park has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Quelagetting scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Queens Park scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Quelagetting scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Queens Park or Quelagetting?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Queens Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
